summary: Pre-Raphaelite Brotherhood painter. Best known for The Light of the World, The Awakening Conscience, The Scapegoat, and The Hireling Shepherd. Co-founder of the PRB with Millais and Rossetti. Painted religious subjects with intense naturalistic detail.

works.name: The Light of the World (1851-53)
works.description: Christ knocks on a weed-choked door with no outside handle, holding a lantern. At Keble College, Oxford.

clues.clue: Christ holds a lantern in his left hand and knocks with his right at a door overgrown with roots/thorns/weeds; no handle on outside (can only be opened from inside)
examples: Christ carries a lantern and knocks on a door overgrown with weeds
a lavishly dressed Jesus knocks at a door overgrown with roots and thorns
the lack of a handle on the door in this painting indicates it could only be opened from the inside

clues.clue: Repainted when he discovered Keble College, Oxford was charging sixpence to see it
examples: repainted his most famous work when he discovered that sixpence was being charged to see it by Keble College, Oxford

clues.clue: Title from John 8: 'Whoever follows me will not walk in darkness'; painting illustrates Revelation metaphor
examples: this painting's title is derived from John 8, in which Jesus tells a crowd that 'Whoever follows me will not walk in darkness'

works.name: The Awakening Conscience (1853)
works.description: A woman rises/leaps from the lap of her lover sitting in a blue smoking jacket by a piano. Intensely symbolic interior details.

clues.clue: Woman rises/leaps from the lap of her lover (man in blue smoking jacket) in front of/by a piano; pink bow visible
examples: a woman rising from her lover's lap in front of a piano
a man in blue smoking jacket lazes in a chair by a piano, while his mistress leaps out of his lap
a woman with a pink bow sitting on a man's lap in front of a piano

clues.clue: A musical arrangement of Tennyson's 'Tears, Idle Tears' lies discarded on the floor
examples: A musical arrangement of Tennyson's Tears, Idle Tears lies discarded on the floor

clues.clue: A solitary yellow glove on the rug next to a tangled, unraveling skein of yarn
examples: a solitary yellow glove lies on the rug next to a tangled, unraveling skein of yarn

clues.clue: Under the table, a greedy-eyed cat plays with a dead bird
examples: under a table on the left, a greedy-eyed cat plays with a dead bird

clues.clue: Blue wall design featuring dancing women bearing globes
examples: a blue wall design featuring dancing women bearing globes

works.name: The Scapegoat (1854-55)
works.description: A goat with a red ribbon around its horns dying in the skeleton-littered salt shallows of the Dead Sea. Based on Leviticus.

clues.clue: Title animal with red ribbon around its horns, dying in skeleton-littered salt shallows of the Dead Sea
examples: The title animal has a red ribbon wrapped around its (*) horns and is dying in the skeleton-littered salt shallows of the Dead Sea
his depiction of a woman rising from the lap of her lover in The Awakening Conscience

clues.clue: Attempted to synthesize Judaism and Christianity; draws from Leviticus
examples: Hunt attempted to convert adherents of this religion to Christianity by synthesizing the two in The Scapegoat, which draws from a practice detailed in Leviticus

works.name: The Hireling Shepherd (1851)
works.description: A man in a wheat field shows a death's-head hawkmoth to a girl (modeled after Emma Watkins).

clues.clue: Man shows a death's-head hawkmoth to a girl in a wheat field; modeled after local youth Emma Watkins
examples: the central figure shows a girl a death's-head hawk moth

works.name: The Shadow of Death (1870-73)
works.description: Jesus stretches his arms in front of carpentry tools, creating a premonition of the crucifixion. Mary opens a box with Magi's gifts.

clues.clue: Jesus stretches arms upward in front of a rack of carpentry tools, casting a shadow that prefigures the crucifixion; Mary opens box with Magi's gifts
examples: depicts Jesus stretching his arms upward in front of a rack of carpentry tools while standing amid wood shavings

comprehensive_summary:
William Holman Hunt was a founding member of the Pre-Raphaelite Brotherhood (with John Everett Millais and Dante Gabriel Rossetti), known for intensely detailed religious and moralistic paintings. His most iconic work, The Light of the World (1851-53), shows Christ holding a lantern and knocking at a door overgrown with thorns and weeds, with no outside handle (symbolizing that it can only be opened from within). Based on John 8 and Revelation, it hangs at Keble College, Oxford, where Hunt repainted it after discovering sixpence was being charged to view it.

The Awakening Conscience (1853) depicts a woman rising from the lap of her lover (a man in a blue smoking jacket) by a piano, filled with symbolic details: a musical arrangement of Tennyson's 'Tears, Idle Tears' discarded on the floor, a yellow glove on the rug next to a tangled skein of yarn, a greedy-eyed cat playing with a dead bird under the table, and a blue wall design with dancing women bearing globes.

The Scapegoat (1854-55) shows a goat with a red ribbon around its horns dying in the skeleton-littered salt shallows of the Dead Sea, drawing from Leviticus and attempting to synthesize Judaism and Christianity. The Hireling Shepherd (1851) depicts a man showing a death's-head hawkmoth to a girl modeled after local youth Emma Watkins. The Shadow of Death shows Jesus stretching his arms before carpentry tools, prefiguring the crucifixion, while Mary opens a box of the Magi's gifts. His Lady of Shalott includes a split mirror and unwinding thread, worked on with Edward Robert Hughes. He also painted Isabella and the Pot of Basil (showing Isabella after Lorenzo's death, unlike Millais's version) and A Converted British Family Sheltering a Christian Missionary as a companion to Millais's Christ in the House of His Parents.
